steps.title: Understand the relationship between the two sentences
steps.content: Sentence 1 describes what happens when oxygen is drawn in: a chemical reaction produces a glow. The blank begins a sentence about what happens when oxygen stops being drawn in: the reaction and the glow cease. These are opposite conditions, so the transition must signal contrast — not example, elaboration, or conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate three choices, confirm one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'For instance,': Signals that an example of the previous idea follows. The second sentence is not an example of glowing — it is the reverse scenario. ✗
  • Choice B — 'By contrast,': Signals a direct opposition between two conditions. Oxygen in $\Rightarrow$ glow on; oxygen out $\Rightarrow$ glow off. This is precisely a contrast. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Specifically,': Signals added detail or elaboration on the previous point. The second sentence reverses rather than elaborates. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In conclusion,': Signals a summary of what came before. The second sentence introduces a new contrasting condition, not a wrap-up. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
